qa_pairs.question: Is uv supported on the clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: No, uv is currently not supported on our clusters.

qa_pairs.question: What is uv?
qa_pairs.answer: uv is an extremely fast Python package and project manager written in Rust.

qa_pairs.question: Can uv be used to install all Python packages on the clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: While uv may be able to install some packages, it will likely fail for others.

qa_pairs.question: What are some common issues or pitfalls when trying to use uv on the clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Some issues include uv attempting to install packages incompatible with our clusters, uv being unaware of Python packages provided by loaded modules, and uv quickly filling up your /home directory quota because it stores many files in its cache.

qa_pairs.question: Why might uv quickly fill up my /home directory quota?
qa_pairs.answer: uv can quickly fill up your /home directory quota because it stores a very large number of files in its cache.

qa_pairs.question: Does uv recognize Python packages that come from loaded modules?
qa_pairs.answer: No, uv is unaware of the Python packages provided by loaded modules.

qa_pairs.question: What tool should be used to install Python packages on our clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: To install packages on our clusters, use `pip`.

qa_pairs.question: What version of pip is recommended for package installation?
qa_pairs.answer: You should make sure to use at least `pip>=25.0`.

qa_pairs.question: How do I upgrade pip to the recommended version?
qa_pairs.answer: You can upgrade pip using the command `pip install --no-index --upgrade pip`.

qa_pairs.question: What command is used to clear the uv cache?
qa_pairs.answer: To clear the uv cache, use the command `uv cache clean`.

qa_pairs.question: How can I prevent uv from using its cache?
qa_pairs.answer: To avoid using the cache, use `uv --no-cache`.
